headline: Hurricane Warning issued for Sample County — Category 3 making landfall in 14 hours
description: Hurricane Helena, currently a Category 3 storm with maximum sustained winds of 125 mph, is forecast to make landfall near the Sample County coastline between 0200-0600 UTC tomorrow. Storm surge of 8-12 feet is expected in coastal zones. Life-threatening conditions will begin 6 hours before landfall. Tornadoes are possible in outer bands.
instruction: ALL residents in coastal and low-lying areas (Zones A, E) must evacuate NOW. Do not wait for official evacuation order if you are in a surge zone. Shelter in place inland residents in Zone B-D in a safe room or interior room away from windows.
area_desc: Sample County, North Valley, South Flats, Downtown Core, Hillside District

headline: Potentially life-threatening storm surge of 8-12 feet above ground level
description: Storm surge of 8 to 12 feet above ground level is possible for the Sample County coastline and tidal waterways. This surge could penetrate up to 3 miles inland in Zone E (South Flats). Water levels may rise quickly and the surge will be accompanied by large and dangerous waves.
instruction: Leave immediately if you live or work in surge prone areas. Do not attempt to drive through flooded roads.
area_desc: Sample County Coastline, Zone E (South Flats)

headline: Tornado Watch for inland portions of Sample County through 2200 UTC
description: Isolated tornadoes are possible in the outer bands of Hurricane Helena, particularly in the inland zones. Tornadoes associated with tropical systems can occur with little warning.
instruction: Monitor local radio and TV for warnings. Be ready to move to a safe room immediately if a warning is issued.
area_desc: Zone B (Downtown Core), Zone C (Hillside District)
